rationale: The US-Iran settlement and Xi's visit to Pyongyang are qualitatively new geopolitical shifts: the former may affect US attention and resources available for Ukraine, the latter signals deepening of the axis supporting Russia.

top_events.title: Trump says US and Iran reached a 'great settlement'
top_events.summary: A US diplomatic win with Iran may shift Trump's foreign policy focus and political capital away from Ukraine support, raising uncertainty about continued Western backing — a meaningful new development for Ukrainians.

top_events.title: Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang to revitalise China-North Korea ties
top_events.summary: Deepening China-North Korea alignment strengthens the axis that supports Russia with materiel and diplomatic cover, a direct negative signal for Ukraine's war effort.

top_events.title: Vance says Israeli PM Netanyahu 'has got some things wrong'
top_events.summary: US VP publicly criticising a key ally signals a more transactional US foreign policy posture, adding to Ukrainian uncertainty about long-term US commitment.
